description: Test corpus for the frontend-security skill. It is schema-driven because no deterministic gate scanner judges these fixtures — a model reads each snippet against the skill's ALWAYS/NEVER rules; 'detect' means a browser-side sink (innerHTML, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, eval) is fed untrusted user/URL data and produces XSS (CWE-79), while 'ignore' means the secure fix using framework escaping / textContent / a DOMPurify sanitizer so the same data is rendered inertly.

fixtures.reason: Assigns a query-string value into innerHTML, so q='' executes as script. Violates NEVER 'use innerHTML= with untrusted input'. CWE-79 reflected DOM XSS.
Uses textContent, which assigns the value as inert text — any markup in q is shown literally, not parsed. Follows ALWAYS 'treat URL data as untrusted, render via escaping'. No CWE-79 sink.

Renders user-authored comment.body via dangerouslySetInnerHTML with no sanitization, so a comment containing <script>/onerror markup executes. Violates NEVER 'use dangerouslySetInnerHTML with untrusted input'. CWE-79 stored XSS.

Passes the untrusted body through DOMPurify with a strict HTML profile before rendering, stripping script and event-handler vectors. Follows ALWAYS 'for raw HTML use a vetted sanitizer (DOMPurify) with a strict allowlist'. Mitigates CWE-79.

Passes a user-supplied string straight to eval, allowing arbitrary JavaScript execution (in the browser this is DOM XSS; here arbitrary code execution). Violates NEVER 'use eval with untrusted input'. CWE-79 / CWE-95 code injection.

Replaces eval with a sandboxed math expression evaluator and an arithmetic-only input allowlist, so no user string is ever interpreted as JavaScript. Follows ALWAYS 'treat user data as untrusted' and avoids the eval NEVER rule. No code-injection sink (CWE-79/CWE-95).
